purpose: Reference registry for external game engines, remakes, public game catalogs, and asset sources that may inform Prismtek-owned implementations.

defaultPolicy.shipPolicy: Do not ship external code, binaries, art, audio, fonts, models, or data from this registry until license and content ownership are reviewed for the exact files being used.
defaultPolicy.preferredUse: Use as local research references, then build original Prismtek code and assets.

references.name: Awesome Game Remakes
references.licenseObserved: CC BY-SA 4.0 for the list
references.notes: Catalog says most games require original assets; each linked project must be reviewed separately.

references.name: Open Source Game Clones
references.licenseObserved: not reviewed in repo
references.notes: Use as discovery only; every listed project needs separate license and content review.

references.name: Godot Engine
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Best broad open engine candidate for Prismtek 2D/3D prototypes, desktop, mobile, and RGDS Android/Linux experiments.

references.name: Phaser
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Strong fit for Prismtek browser arcade upgrades and web-first game loops.

references.name: raylib
references.licenseObserved: zlib/libpng per upstream project materials
references.notes: Excellent low-resource C engine candidate for arcade, desktop, Linux handheld, and lightweight tool experiments.

references.name: Bevy
references.licenseObserved: MIT OR Apache-2.0 per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful for ECS, simulation, AI, and future Rust-native Prismtek systems; treat as research until stability and platform fit are proven.

references.name: libGDX
references.licenseObserved: Apache-2.0 per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful Java/Kotlin-adjacent reference for Android, desktop, and handheld runtime comparisons.

references.name: LÖVE
references.licenseObserved: zlib-style license for LÖVE core; bundled dependencies vary
references.notes: Good Lua framework for small arcade prototypes and handheld-style experiments.

references.name: HaxeFlixel
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Strong 2D pixel-game framework for browser/native builds and arcade-style prototypes.

references.name: MonoGame
references.licenseObserved: Microsoft Public License per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful C#/XNA-style reference for 2D/3D structure and desktop/mobile builds.

references.name: Flame
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Good candidate for Flutter/mobile-first minigames and BeMore-adjacent game surfaces.

references.name: GDevelop
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials; service/editor assets may vary
references.notes: Useful for visual/event-based game authoring research and rapid prototype workflows.

references.name: Open 3D Engine
references.licenseObserved: Apache-2.0 per upstream project materials
references.notes: Heavyweight 3D engine for long-term 3D/simulation research, not a first-choice arcade shipping path.

references.name: Stride
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: C# 2D/3D engine reference for desktop/mobile/VR exploration.

references.name: OGRE
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Rendering engine reference only; not a complete Prismtek game engine path by itself.

references.name: Solarus
references.licenseObserved: GPL family per upstream project materials; verify exact current terms before use
references.notes: Useful for 2D action-RPG patterns; copyleft terms mean adoption must be intentional.

references.name: Defold
references.licenseObserved: custom/source-available terms per upstream project materials; verify before use
references.notes: Potentially useful for cross-platform 2D games, but treat as cautious reference until licensing and distribution terms are reviewed.

references.name: Cocos2d-x
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Mature C++ 2D engine reference for cross-platform architecture; evaluate before new adoption.

references.name: PixiJS
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Strong browser rendering candidate for Prismtek arcade UI, effects, particles, and 2D scenes.

references.name: three.js
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful for browser 3D experiments, product scenes, and lightweight 3D arcade prototypes.

references.name: Babylon.js
references.licenseObserved: Apache-2.0 per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful for browser 3D, WebXR, and richer product/game visualization experiments.

references.name: PlayCanvas Engine
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Open web 3D engine reference; editor/cloud workflow should be reviewed separately.

references.name: Panda3D
references.licenseObserved: BSD-style license per upstream project materials
references.notes: Useful for Python-friendly 3D simulations, tools, and AI/game research.

references.name: jMonkeyEngine
references.licenseObserved: BSD-style license per upstream project materials
references.notes: Java 3D engine reference for Android/desktop architecture comparisons.

references.name: Urho3D
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Lightweight C++ 2D/3D engine reference for compact desktop/mobile experiments.

references.name: Fyrox
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials
references.notes: Rust game engine reference for 2D/3D and editor-driven workflows.

references.name: macroquad
references.licenseObserved: MIT OR Apache-2.0 per upstream project materials
references.notes: Tiny Rust framework candidate for simple 2D arcade and handheld-style prototypes.

references.name: Ren'Py
references.licenseObserved: MIT per upstream project materials; bundled dependencies vary
references.notes: Useful for narrative systems, dialogue-heavy game modes, and visual novel product experiments.

references.name: Minetest / Luanti
references.licenseObserved: LGPL family per upstream project materials; games/assets vary separately
references.notes: Useful for voxel/world simulation and open-world survival reference; bundled games/assets require separate review.

references.name: OpenRA
references.licenseObserved: GPL family per upstream project materials; original game assets not included for shipping reuse
references.notes: Useful RTS architecture reference; avoid franchise assets and intentional copyleft adoption issues.

references.name: SuperTuxKart
references.licenseObserved: GPL family for code; assets may vary
references.notes: Useful kart/racing reference for future Prismtek racer ideas; do not vendor assets without review.

references.name: The Battle for Wesnoth
references.licenseObserved: GPL family per upstream project materials; assets may vary
references.notes: Useful turn-based strategy architecture reference; adoption would require copyleft review.

references.name: Veloren
references.licenseObserved: GPL family per upstream project materials; verify exact current terms before use
references.notes: Useful open-world multiplayer RPG reference; do not vendor code/assets without intentional license review.

references.name: T-Rex Runner Game Nintendo DS
references.licenseObserved: no license file found during review
references.notes: Useful for devkitPro Nintendo DS structure; do not ship its code or assets without explicit permission.

references.name: TerrariaDS
references.licenseObserved: custom non-commercial license
references.notes: Useful for tile world and survival mechanics; do not ship in commercial Prismtek distributions.

references.name: Minicraft DS Edition
references.licenseObserved: no license file found during review
references.notes: Useful for dual-screen survival UI patterns; do not ship its code or assets without explicit permission.

references.name: Castagne Engine
references.licenseObserved: MPL 2.0 for engine source per project materials; assets and docs vary by folder
references.notes: Evaluate for Pixel Fruit Arena combat architecture before adoption.

references.name: OpenBOR
references.licenseObserved: BSD-style terms in LICENSE
references.notes: Good for original Prismtek side-scrolling brawler modules; community modules require separate review.

references.name: Ikemen GO
references.licenseObserved: MIT engine; bundled screenpack assets and FFmpeg have separate terms
references.notes: Good for traditional 2D fighter experiments; MUGEN-format content must not be assumed safe.

references.name: TORCS
references.licenseObserved: GPL v2 source with some non-free artwork directories noted by project README
references.notes: Useful for racing AI and simulation architecture; avoid vendoring bundled data/artwork.

references.name: Screaming Brain Studios
references.licenseObserved: CC0 / Public Domain stated by source site
references.notes: Good candidate for original Prismtek assets after pack-level provenance is recorded.

references.name: OpenGameArt
references.licenseObserved: per-asset licensing
references.notes: Record exact asset page, author, license, attribution text, and derivative chain before committing files.

references.name: Kenney Assets
references.licenseObserved: CC0 stated by source site for many packs; verify each pack
references.notes: High-value 2D/3D/UI/audio game asset source; record exact pack and license before committing.

references.name: itch.io Game Assets
references.licenseObserved: per-asset licensing
references.notes: Large source for pixel packs and UI kits; each page has its own license and author terms.

references.name: CraftPix Freebies
references.licenseObserved: per-pack licensing
references.notes: Useful for 2D characters, tiles, UI, and effects; verify commercial and attribution terms per pack.

references.name: GameDev Market
references.licenseObserved: per-asset licensing
references.notes: Marketplace source; record license, receipt, and author terms before committing.

references.name: Lospec
references.licenseObserved: per-resource licensing
references.notes: Useful for palettes, pixel-art references, and community resources; review each resource.

references.name: Game-icons.net
references.licenseObserved: CC BY 3.0 per source materials; verify current attribution requirements
references.notes: Excellent icon source for UI and status effects; attribution records are required.

references.name: Google Fonts
references.licenseObserved: per-font licensing, commonly OFL or Apache-2.0
references.notes: Use for UI fonts after recording exact family, license, and source URL.

references.name: Open Font Library
references.licenseObserved: per-font licensing
references.notes: Useful open font source; each font requires license and attribution review.

references.name: Poly Haven
references.licenseObserved: CC0 stated by source site; verify asset page
references.notes: Useful for HDRIs, textures, and 3D models; record asset URL and license page before committing.

references.name: ambientCG
references.licenseObserved: CC0 stated by source site; verify asset page
references.notes: Useful for textures/materials in 3D or stylized scenes; record source asset page.

references.name: Quaternius
references.licenseObserved: CC0/Public Domain style terms stated by source site; verify pack page
references.notes: Great for low-poly characters, creatures, vehicles, props, and game prototypes.

references.name: Sketchfab
references.licenseObserved: per-model licensing
references.notes: Use only models with explicit compatible license and attribution records.

references.name: CGTrader Free 3D Models
references.licenseObserved: per-model licensing
references.notes: Marketplace source; review every model license and redistribution terms.

references.name: Freesound
references.licenseObserved: per-sound licensing
references.notes: Useful for SFX; record exact sound URL, author, license, and attribution.

references.name: FreePD
references.licenseObserved: public-domain style source; verify track page
references.notes: Good for prototype background music after track-level provenance is recorded.

references.name: Incompetech
references.licenseObserved: license varies by track/download terms; often attribution required
references.notes: Useful music source; capture track, author, license, and attribution exactly.

references.name: Audionautix
references.licenseObserved: license terms require review; attribution commonly required
references.notes: Music source candidate; verify current terms and attribution before use.

references.name: ZapSplat
references.licenseObserved: source-specific license and account terms
references.notes: Useful SFX source but account, attribution, and redistribution terms must be reviewed before committing files.

references.name: Sonniss GDC Game Audio Bundles
references.licenseObserved: bundle-specific license terms
references.notes: High-value game audio bundles; store license text and provenance before committing any extracted sounds.

references.name: Pixabay
references.licenseObserved: source-specific content license; verify media page
references.notes: Useful for photos, sounds, and music references; exact asset terms must be recorded.

references.name: Mixkit
references.licenseObserved: source-specific licenses by category; verify asset page
references.notes: Useful for music, SFX, video, and template references; capture category license.

references.name: Pexels
references.licenseObserved: source-specific media license; verify asset page
references.notes: Use for mood/reference imagery or backgrounds only after reviewing the exact media page and model/property risks.

references.name: Unsplash
references.licenseObserved: source-specific media license; verify asset page
references.notes: Useful for reference/mood imagery; review exact terms and avoid implying model/property releases.

references.name: LibreGameAssets / FreeGameDev assets
references.licenseObserved: catalog/discovery; linked resources vary
references.notes: Use as discovery only; every linked source needs individual review.

references.name: Liberated Pixel Cup
references.licenseObserved: mixed copyleft/attribution terms; verify exact asset terms
references.notes: Useful style/reference source; share-alike/copyleft implications require careful review before use.
